hawkish_phrases: domestic price pressures were still high, services inflation was elevated and headline inflation was likely to remain above the target well into next year
the last mile of disinflation was more challenging and that the task of bringing inflation down sustainably to the 2% target was not yet assured
services inflation remained the central element shaping the inflation outlook...progress in reducing services inflation appeared to have stalled
would keep policy rates sufficiently restrictive for as long as necessary to achieve this aim
current distribution of inflation expectations was still skewed to the upside
dovish_phrases: the short-term outlook for growth had deteriorated...weak PMI indicators for manufacturing
weaker activity was likely to dampen inflation over time
the inflationary impact of high wage growth had been buffered by profits
the transmission of monetary policy restriction was likely to have passed its peak
risks to economic growth were tilted to the downside
summary: The ECB Governing Council unanimously held all three key policy rates unchanged in July 2024, maintaining a restrictive stance with cautious rhetoric emphasizing persistent domestic price pressures and elevated services inflation despite acknowledging deteriorating growth conditions and gradual disinflation progress.
